claim_ids: INGEST-CLAIM-001
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment: A narrower Rust-only implementation is proven, but the claim is materially overbroad and assigns ownership imprecisely. At nu_plugin commit 9451b20e42d79f72d4fa5b2a190a39bd1d9d5e5a, Nushell is the plugin host/table cockpit; Rust code recursively enumerates the selected root, selects files classified as Rust source, reads them as UTF-8, parses them with syn, and emits syntax-only rows including functions and structs. Cargo dependency rows are obtained separately by invoking `cargo metadata --format-version 1`, not mapped from the Rust AST. The active profile-owned CodeDB 0.1.0 binary reproduced the function/struct rows and captured real symlink metadata, but it also traversed `.git` (437 rows) and `target` (13,738 rows), so ignored/generated boundaries are classified rather than excluded. Polyglot AST support remains documented as candidates/planning, not current implementation. The complete required fixture corpus was not executable under the strict no-write constraint because there is no committed malformed-Rust fixture and the dependency fixture has no Cargo.lock while the product's metadata command lacks `--locked`; therefore the exact broad claim is qualified, not verified.

claim_ids: INGEST-CLAIM-002
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment: Current primary evidence contradicts the exact implementation claim. The current nu_plugin_codedb surface declares Type::Nothing input, every inspected command run method ignores `_input`, and the plugin crate has no codedb-store-redb/redb dependency; it produces native Nushell table values from repository/path arguments instead of receiving structured blocks. The pinned redb 4.1.0 writer exists in a separate codedb CLI path and persists `(relative_path, bytes)` batches scanned from the filesystem. Existing redb tests cover durable byte batches, checkpoint resume, future-schema refusal, and writer contention, but there is no plugin-output-to-redb call path or structured-block schema to replay. Installed profile ownership also keeps separate `codedb` and `nu_plugin_codedb` binaries; binary probes found redb writer symbols only in `codedb`, while the plugin contains the explicit text `redb store is not implemented until CDB015`. No write-producing fixture/test command was run because this pass was strictly read-only.

claim_ids: INGEST-CLAIM-003
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment: Current primary evidence contradicts the asserted envctl pipeline. Envctl's contract forbids reading CodeDB redb internals; its default-off semantic module reads exported JSONL rows that already contain embedding_minilm vectors and builds only an in-memory HNSW index. No tracked caller of SemanticIndex was found. Envctl's ruvector-postgres dependency is an optional, default-off server-side extension, explicitly not a PostgreSQL client. A separate CodeDB implementation in the nu_plugin repository can capture raw file bytes directly to either redb or PostgreSQL through alternative BlobStore backends, but its PostgreSQL table explicitly omits the optional embedding column and its parity test independently writes the same fixture to both stores rather than transferring redb blocks to PostgreSQL. No envctl redb-to-PostgreSQL call path, embedding-generation owner/model, transaction receipt, or repeat/recovery run exists in the inspected evidence. Installed behavior was not proven: envctl is absent from PATH and from /home/flexnetos/.nix-profile/bin; only a source-build symlink exists under /home/flexnetos/meta/usr/bin, which is not an active profile frontdoor.

claim_ids: INGEST-CLAIM-004
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment: The core statement is proven only as captured source framing: NBSOURCE-030 presents parser, redb buffer, and redb-to-PostgreSQL bridge atoms and summarizes them as a Phase 1 three-step flat-file-to-database proposal. It is not yet a committed, accepted, or implemented pipeline. Reconciliation against the committed graph is: (1) flat files -> AST blocks: FOUNDATION-001 must pin the sole parser/runtime package owner and POSTGRES-004 must define a read-only, bounded collector with stable source identity, byte/metadata hashes, AST schema, provenance, ordering, checkpoints, failure recovery, and lossless reconstruction; gate on a pinned parser plus deterministic fixture corpus and failure receipts. (2) AST blocks -> redb staging: require a versioned block schema, exact record hashes, ordered/idempotent writes, deduplication, crash replay, recovery source, and an explicit transient-versus-durable authority decision; gate on ordered/duplicate/malformed/interrupted/concurrent fixtures and STORE-001/PGAUTH-002 owner approval. This stage is not adequately represented by the claim's four aggregate refs and instead appears in adjacent draft tasks PGAUTH-002, POSTGRES-006, STORE-001, and the INGEST-CLAIM-002 queue row. (3) redb -> embedded PostgreSQL: require selected-record identity, pinned embedding model/configuration, transactional row/vector receipts, provenance, repeat/recovery behavior, and one-way authority into PostgreSQL; gate on a bounded transaction test, vector dimension/hash receipts, and deterministic round-trip projection. POSTGRES-008 is specifically the reverse database-to-directory projection contract, not the forward ingestion bridge, so POSTGRES-005/006/009 and PGAUTH-004 are also needed for the complete bridge/round-trip. Final gate: owner-reviewed pipeline graph, committed stage contracts, fixture-based end-to-end proof, explicit acceptance, then LIFEOS-011 only after all child proofs pass. All four canonical aggregate tasks are currently Draft and their planned artifacts/proofs are absent.

claim_ids: AUTH-CLAIM-001
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment:
The exact authority claim is refuted by current local primary evidence. PostgreSQL is an implemented optional direct CodeDB backend, but envctl is not the synchronizing writer in that path: CodeDB selects either redb (the default durable backend) or PostgreSQL and calls the selected backend's persist_batch directly. The PostgreSQL implementation performs its own INSERT/ON CONFLICT transaction into the dedicated codebase_codedb table and deliberately avoids the production codebase table. Envctl manages the PostgreSQL/RuVector component lifecycle and consumes already-embedded CodeDB export rows into an in-memory index; the inspected envctl path neither generates those embeddings nor writes ingested blocks/embeddings to PostgreSQL. The planning graph expresses a future PostgreSQL macro-authority/projection design, but every canonical task cited by this claim is Draft, the required authority artifacts are absent, and the claim queue remains queued with Ready empty.

Authority matrix (current evidence):
Layer | Authorized write trace | Denied, absent, or unproven trace
file | CodeDB materialization may emit derived files from a selected store. | Capture is read-only on the scanned tree; source files are not an authorized capture write target.
redb | Default CodeDB capture target; persist_batch commits each batch durably. Envctl separately owns a migration-ledger redb, not an ingestion bridge. | Redb is not proven to be merely transient, and no redb-to-envctl-to-PostgreSQL flush path was found.
envctl | Starts/stops/verifies the PostgreSQL/RuVector component and builds an in-memory semantic index from exported rows that already carry vectors. | No current block-ingestion DML writer, embedding generator, or redb-to-PostgreSQL synchronizer was found. Reverse synchronization from projections is explicitly forbidden by the Draft PGAUTH-004 target contract.
embedding | Existing embedding_minilm values from CodeDB export may be loaded into envctl's in-memory RuVector index. | The inspected envctl path skips rows without vectors and does not generate or persist embeddings; CodeDB PgStore's current DML omits an embedding column.
PostgreSQL | CodeDB may directly select PgStore and UPSERT raw file blobs in one transaction into a dedicated table. | It is not proven as the sole/permanent authority: redb remains a durable default, PgStore avoids production codebase, the owner-approved authority contract is missing, and no installed write trace was permitted or available.

Authorized current trace: source file --fs::read--> CodeDB capture --open selected backend--> redb persist_batch/commit OR PgStore INSERT...ON CONFLICT/commit. Denied or absent claimed trace: source/redb --envctl read+embed+sync--> PostgreSQL. Planned target trace (not current proof): PostgreSQL branch/version --envctl one-way projection--> disposable directory/build input; reverse writes from projection to canonical state are forbidden.

claim_ids: ENVPROJ-CLAIM-002
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment: At envctl commit fd3d423fff767b4b96f67fdc46a8aac93da21559, the implemented `catalog render` path does not query PostgreSQL or select a production branch. CLI dispatch calls `Engine::catalog_render`, which calls `catalog::render`; `render` builds its snapshot by scanning repo/manifest files and the in-memory Registry, then writes deterministic projections. The current render request/report has no branch identity, database version, selector authority, authorization, audit-event, or rollback fields. PostgreSQL material found locally is limited to an optional, default-off server-side RuVector extension declaration and separate health/embedding-count psql probes; neither participates in projection. LifeOS primary tables explicitly mark the NotebookLM wording as source-only/unverified and queued, while POSTGRES-008 remains a Draft definition task. No matching integration test exists in current test directories, and no installed `envctl` binary/profile entry was available, so installed behavior was not proven. Owner decision and the complete selection/authorization/pinning/failure/audit/rollback contract remain absent.

claim_ids: ENVPROJ-CLAIM-003
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment: Current primary evidence contradicts the claim. In envctl, the only tracked use of `module_path` is the default-off RuVector semantic consumer: it parses exported JSONL and stores `module_path` as an HNSW vector id/metadata, not as an in-memory directory tree. The actual file reconstruction implementation is owned by CodeDB in the independent `nu_plugin` repository: `codedb materialize` lists stored `SourceFileRow.relative_path` rows, joins each path directly to `out_dir`, and writes files to disk. PostgreSQL happens to store that relative path in its `module_path` column, but the materializer is CodeDB, not envctl, and it does not reconstruct an exact directory structure in memory. The broader exact-reconstruction wording is also false: there is no materialization-time path normalization or traversal/absolute-path refusal; duplicate keys update/overwrite rather than refuse; directories are inferred only as file parents (empty directories are not restored); symlinks are capture gaps; batch capture records no file mode; and tests cover checksum/byte round trips, one executable-bit case, and backend path/hash parity, not exact whole-tree reconstruction. CodeDB export rows that name envctl as a possible materialization owner are declarative (`runtime_status=declared`) and have no current envctl consumer. Relevant tracked source files had empty diffs. During the read-only pass, unrelated concurrent changes appeared under envctl `home/.claude/`; `nu_plugin` already had untracked `.claude/`. No repository or runtime was edited by this verification.

claim_ids: ENVPROJ-CLAIM-004
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment: Current primary evidence contradicts the claim. envctl has no raw_code block schema, block identity, ordering key, concatenation path, or Rust-file projection. Its current catalog is explicitly an in-memory/file-first bridge whose renderer emits a fixed set of generated catalog/config files (including two TOML projections, but no Rust source), and its optional CodeDB semantic consumer deserializes only module_path plus embedding_minilm while ignoring extra fields. The exact-byte file materializer found in the workspace belongs to CodeDB/nu_plugin, and it restores whole SHA-256-addressed source blobs rather than concatenating raw_code blocks. CodeDB's exported envctl integration records say envctl's role is declared/downstream; they do not implement the consumer in envctl. Existing envctl tests cover deterministic fixed catalog projections and secret redaction for catalog environment rows, not raw-block reconstruction, duplicate/partial-block behavior, encoding, transactional failure cleanup, or Rust/TOML byte parity. No profile-owned envctl frontdoor exists; an explicit workspace-owned binary was inspected read-only and exposed catalog tables with zero codedb_file_imports rows for the envctl repo.

The active HOME Nushell `plugin list` returned `[]`; generated Yazelix Nushell initializers contain no CodeDB registration/use; searches of active Codex/agent configuration found no agent-to-native-plugin command path; and the repository's Codex bridge directs agents to bounded CLI/MCP calls instead. Therefore the source's broad current-use wording cannot be verified as written.

claim_ids: YNP-CLAIM-002
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment: Current local primary evidence proves the narrower concrete statement that `nu_plugin_codedb` is a Rust-native Nushell plugin binary: Cargo declares it as a Rust `bin`, its Rust entrypoint implements `nu_plugin::Plugin` and calls `serve_plugin(..., MsgPackSerializer)`, Nix builds and tests it through `rustPlatform.buildRustPackage` with a locked Cargo graph, and the active profile resolves it through the profile-owned Yazelix foundation closure to a verified ELF containing a rustc 1.95.0 marker. The installed binary SHA-256 is `149537bbba39583451d0c33c0313d7a40b618b41b5fa4fcdaefd3b7a613c5127`; its package NAR hash is `sha256-hctmPEcqDD3AS4cJfQSudDAb1LdlolsY0V9tplwFKfg=` and `nix-store --verify-path` succeeded. Qualification is required because NBSOURCE-032 never enumerates the plural set of "referenced Nushell plugins", so only `nu_plugin_codedb` could be provenance-resolved. Also, the active profile is pinned through Yazelix/Yazi assets to nu_plugin commit `532a461...`, while current nu_plugin source is `9451b20...`; the relevant Cargo target, Rust entrypoint, and Nix build definition are unchanged, but Cargo.lock changed. The generated tree under `~/.local/share/yazelix` has no codedb/plugin materialization, and no independent clean rebuild was performed in this read-only pass.

claim_ids: YNP-CLAIM-003
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
version_or_commit: FlexNetOS/lifeos@evidence observed on local main worktree; FlexNetOS/nu_plugin@532a461841316a6623701f9e206b97cafa994d40; nushell/nushell@9c05da49a89c8e7a898be3f712536087a415002a
assessment: The concrete profile-owned `nu_plugin_codedb` path is proven to use MessagePack: Yazelix pins FlexNetOS/nu_plugin commit 532a461841316a6623701f9e206b97cafa994d40; that commit pins Nushell plugin/protocol crates 0.113.1 and calls `serve_plugin(&CodeDbPlugin, MsgPackSerializer)`. The locally cached Nushell source at upstream commit 9c05da49a89c8e7a898be3f712536087a415002a specifies the engine/plugin protocol, implements MessagePack encode/decode, and contains in-memory round-trip fixtures. The active profile resolves both `nu` and `nu_plugin_codedb`; `nu --version` is 0.113.1. A live `--stdio` capture began with the length-prefixed `msgpack` encoder name and decoded to `Hello { protocol: nu-plugin, version: 0.113.1 }`; an isolated installed-Nu invocation then completed a shell-to-plugin-to-shell command round trip and returned a structured CodeDB row. Qualification is required because Nushell 0.113.1 supports both JSON and MessagePack encoders, and NBSOURCE-032 does not identify the broader plural set of 'referenced Nushell plugins'; current primary evidence proves the profile-owned CodeDB plugin, not every unnamed plugin.

claim_ids: YNP-CLAIM-004
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment: Qualified for the pinned serializer path, not the entire plugin protocol. The active profile-owned CodeDB plugin is proven to use Nushell 0.113.1 MsgPackSerializer. A reproducible 200-run benchmark over 1,000 representative structured rows measured MessagePack at p50 355,011 ns versus JSON at p50 3,385,076 ns (0.1049x), with p95 ratio 0.1232x, p99 ratio 0.1151x, and output size ratio 0.8407x. This supports the narrower characterization that the pinned MessagePack serializer is materially faster and smaller for this workload; it does not establish whole-protocol end-to-end optimization.

claim_ids: YNP-CLAIM-005
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment: A narrower statement is verified: the active Yazelix/Nix profile contains nu_plugin_codedb 0.1.0 and Nushell 0.113.1, and a live transient invocation returns typed Nushell table values with pinned command signatures. The asserted streaming/agent architecture is not implemented or proven. Current first-party commands implement SimplePluginCommand, build complete Vec<Row> collections, convert them to Value::list, and paginate only after collection; pinned nu-plugin 0.113.1 then consumes any input stream and wraps the result as PipelineData::value rather than PipelineData::ListStream. No agent-identified producer-consumer trace, backpressure test, stream-ordering test, or mid-stream failure receipt exists in the inspected first-party source/tests. Live checks proved typed tables, schema, deterministic pagination order, and fail-closed invalid pagination, but not streaming. Therefore the source wording is materially broader than current implementation evidence.

claim_ids: YNP-CLAIM-006
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment: Current pinned primary evidence proves a narrower statement, not the source wording as written. The installed/source CodeDB plugin selects MsgPack, and Nushell's pinned plugin framework can decode owned PluginInput messages and represent ListStream/ByteStream pipeline data with acknowledgement-based message-count backpressure. However, every CodeDB command is implemented through SimplePluginCommand: the framework explicitly fully consumes any input stream into an owned Value before the command runs, and CodeDB eagerly collects output rows into Value::List before MsgPack serialization. The implementation therefore proves in-memory materialization behind a streaming-capable protocol, not an end-to-end 'streamed directly into memory' or zero-copy/bounded-memory path. No allocation/copy profile, lifetime-ownership test, or bounded-memory failure test exists locally, so the broader/direct wording remains unverified.

claim_ids: YNP-CLAIM-007
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment: Refuted for the claimed current Yazelix/Nushell architecture. The active profile contains the native CodeDB plugin but exposes no vectorizer, embedding model, RVF/AgentDB/RuVector runtime, or vectorization command in the plugin closure/config/generated surfaces. Current CodeDB plugin commands produce typed rows and do not vectorize them. Reference-only meta-ruvector material contains multiple provider choices and no claim-scoped installed benchmark, so the assertion that this in-memory path is instantly vectorized is false for the current installed architecture.

claim_ids: YNP-CLAIM-008
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment: Qualified by a narrower local capability, not by the claimed native Nushell/Yazelix path. The handoff peer deterministically maps ledger events to 384-dimensional hash-based pseudo-embeddings and best-effort writes them to a gitignored .handoff/ledger.db.rvf sidecar; a concrete local artifact exists. The active profile-owned CodeDB plugin has no RVF writer or vectorizer, and its capture backends are redb or PostgreSQL. meta-ruvector was inspected only as a read-only format/writer reference and was never built, tested, executed, or modified.

claim_ids: YNP-CLAIM-009
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment: A narrower current-source statement is proven: envctl commit fd3d423fff767b4b96f67fdc46a8aac93da21559 contains a default-OFF `ruvector` semantic adapter that reads `embedding_minilm` vectors keyed by `module_path`, constructs pinned `ruvector-core` 2.2.3 `VectorDB`, and calls `insert`; that pinned package enables its redb-backed `storage` feature by default and writes bincode-encoded `Vec<f32>` values to redb table `vectors` under the vector ID in one committed write transaction. The broad source claim is not fully verified as deployed LifeOS behavior: the envctl feature is off by default, no installed envctl frontdoor was found, the active profile `codedb` runtime exposes source-blob tables rather than a vector table, no current dynamic transaction/crash trace was permitted under the hard read-only/no-generated-files constraint, and the required synchronization/authority decision remains Draft with its expected decision/proof artifacts absent. Thus 'vectors can be written to redb' is source-backed for a pinned optional adapter, but redb authority, installed activation, crash/replay guarantees, and whole-system synchronization are unresolved.

claim_ids: YNP-CLAIM-010
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment: The narrow protocol statement is verified, but the source's unbounded wording is not. Current source pins the Nushell plugin boundary to MsgPackSerializer, and an installed-runtime strace captured the encoder preamble `\7msgpack` plus binary MessagePack Call/CallResponse frames; a live negative comparison also showed that plugin `--format json` and `--format csv` both return the same native Nushell table shape, so those flags do not turn the plugin protocol payload into JSON text. However, there is no single current plugin-command-to-store path: plugin scan/export commands return native Values and ignore their signature-only `--store` option, while durable persistence is a separate `codedb capture` CLI path. That CLI passes `(String, Vec<u8>)` batches directly into BlobStore; redb inserts byte slices directly, but PostgreSQL separately constructs JSON text for its metadata jsonb column. In addition, plugin envctl-db compatibility commands explicitly request `--json` and parse JSON, import commands consume JSON artifacts, and CLI exports optionally serialize rows to JSON. Therefore 'no JSON text' is supportable only for the Nushell protocol payload and raw blob-content persistence, not for the whole command/store/export/compatibility surface. Required serializer-level instrumentation and a current pinned machine call graph remain absent, so verified would overstate the evidence.

claim_ids: YNP-CLAIM-011
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment: Verified strictly as a question-claim. The locally retained exact fulltext asks, on line 7, whether to explore how Yazelix and Nushell initialize in the background once a portable release folder is launched; SHA-256, byte count, source ID, and notebook ID match the validated packet. This wording neither asserts that such initialization exists nor authorizes portable-release execution. The separate installed-runtime trace proves the current narrower profile-owned runtime: Yazelix source defines packages.x86_64-linux.lifeos_foundation_yzx, the Nix profile resolves /home/flexnetos/.nix-profile/bin/yzx to /nix/store/rd29...-lifeos-foundation-yzx/bin/yzx, relevant installed launch scripts are hash-identical to current source, generated Nushell config links the packaged config with /home/flexnetos/.config/yazelix/shell_nu.nu, and a live Mars -> Zellij -> yzx/yzx_control -> Nushell tree uses /home/flexnetos/.local/share/yazelix. That runtime evidence does not prove that the Nix store output is the source's undefined 'portable release folder,' and the full failure/shutdown/rollback lifecycle remains unproven.

claim_ids: YNP-CLAIM-012
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment: Verified strictly as a question-claim. The current local primary capture contains the exact source question, "or is there another part of the workflow you would like to review?", which supports the atomic statement that the reader is asked to select another workflow topic. The question grants no task priority, readiness, authority, or execution permission. Canonical handling therefore remains: retain it as a question and select work only through the canonical owner-directed task mechanism. No follow-on workflow was selected in this pass: no explicit owner direction linked to a committed canonical task was supplied, the canonical ready_task_ids list is empty, NBVERIFY-000 is complete, and POSTGRES-009 remains Draft with prerequisites.

claim_ids: YNP-CLAIM-013
evidence_type: local source, test, and installed-runtime truth audit
assessment: Verified as a citation-provenance absence claim from the exact 647-byte staged fulltext. Lines 2 and 6 contain citation markers [1, 2], while the complete seven-line object contains no bibliography, source identities, versions, or cited passages. The missing citation targets verify no technical claim.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-001
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: redb 4.1.0 README and 196 passing upstream library/integration tests prove a pure-Rust embedded key-value library with no database daemon or sidecar. This verdict is about redb itself, not the larger dynamically linked LifeOS closure.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-002
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: Current pinned redb 4.1.0 removed its mmap backend. Its optimized file backend uses positioned file reads/writes, and Database exposes a configurable userspace cache (default 1 GiB). The report's current mmap and operating-system-cache-bypass statement is false.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-003
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: redb documents a zero-copy, BTreeMap-based API and can return borrowed views, but its Value trait explicitly serializes with as_bytes and deserializes with from_bytes. Storage uses copy-on-write B-trees, not std::collections::BTreeMap, so 'native Rust types without serialization' is too broad.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-004
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: redb 4.1.0 documents full ACID transactions and serializable isolation; the complete local redb test suite passed 196 tests including immediate persistence, read isolation, transaction behavior, recovery-sensitive paths, and multithreading.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-005
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: redb 4.1.0 documents MVCC with one writer and multiple concurrent snapshot readers; design documentation and passing concurrency tests prove readers do not block the active writer. Writer-to-writer concurrency remains serialized.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-006
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: The pinned artifact is a Rust library crate linked into applications. It is not a compiler and exposes embedded database APIs.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-007
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: redb's Rust implementation is compatible with a musl/static target in principle, but portability is a whole-closure property. The active CodeDB/Yazelix binaries are dynamically linked glibc ELF files with Nix-store interpreter/RUNPATH entries, and the effective Rust toolchain lacked the musl std target, so the claimed portable closure is not currently proven.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-008
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: redb 4.1.0 is a transactional key-value B-tree engine. It exposes no vector coordinate, geometry, nearest-neighbor, HNSW, or distance-computation API. The current Bun-loaded ruvector Node package owns vector geometry.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-009
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: redb implements no cosine-similarity operation. Cosine is supplied by application or RuVector code; the Bun-loaded ruvector 0.2.34 package exports cosineSimilarity and native vector database APIs.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-010
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: The exact zero-copy-f32-plus-AVX-512 redb path does not exist in redb 4.1.0. A reproducible benchmark measured redb byte reads and commits plus a separate scalar application cosine loop at 384 and 4096 dimensions; microsecond results do not convert that application loop into redb functionality or prove AVX-512 use.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-011
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: The component boundary is correct: embedding models create semantic vectors while redb passively stores and retrieves caller-supplied encoded values. Distance computation and semantic meaning remain external.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-012
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: redb is an in-process single-node embedded store and exposes no network server, replication protocol, or distributed-consensus API. Distribution therefore belongs to application protocols.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-013
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: redb is key-value and has no SQL or automatic relational secondary-index facility. Qualification is required because it supports named tables, range scans, and multimap tables from which an application can explicitly build indexes.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-014
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: The local benchmark proves low redb latency on this workstation (1536-byte read p50 about 0.27 microseconds; Immediate commit p50 about 20-24 microseconds), but no claim-scoped LMDB comparator, edge-node hardware run, determinism definition, or crash/sync matrix exists. Competitive-with-LMDB remains unproved.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-015
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: redb can technically implement an embedded high-frequency scratchpad, and measured local operations are fast enough for that use class. No accepted LifeOS data-class, retention, privacy, promotion, or recovery decision makes this its current exclusive role.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-016
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: redb can store an embedding under a BLAKE3-derived key, so the mechanism is feasible. No current model-aware cache schema or implementation proves that content, model, version, configuration, invalidation, and collision handling are all covered.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-017
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: redb can back a durable application outbox or replay queue, but it is not PostgreSQL WAL and no current ordered/idempotent redb-to-PostgreSQL outage protocol is implemented. WAL, outbox, queue, buffer, and authority semantics must not be conflated.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-018
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: Current CodeDB Rust code can transform supported source files into structured syntax rows and Nushell can host those rows. It is not a universal Nushell-owned AST transformer: language coverage varies and Cargo dependency rows come from cargo metadata rather than the Rust AST.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-019
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: At canonical nu_plugin origin/master 9451b20, the Rust plugin uses the Nushell MessagePack protocol but returns typed table values and has no direct redb dependency. Durable redb capture is a separate codedb CLI/BlobStore path, so no direct plugin-MessagePack-to-redb stream exists.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-020
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: An intermediate redb ingestion buffer is a technically possible target, but it is not the universal current role. Current CodeDB uses redb as a durable default source-blob store and envctl uses redb separately for migration/state; authority must be decided per data class.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-021
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: envctl commit c911286 reads exported JSONL rows that already contain embedding_minilm vectors into an in-memory RuVector index. It neither pulls CodeDB redb blocks, generates MiniLM embeddings, nor commits code and vectors to PostgreSQL.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-022
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: Reproducible local evidence supports the redb microsecond tier for the measured byte-read and Immediate-commit operations. No equivalent PostgreSQL-plus-RuVector relational/GNN benchmark was run, and Bun-loaded ruvector itself exposes native sub-millisecond vector operations, so the report's global microsecond-versus-millisecond boundary is not established.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-023
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: RuVector includes a native pgrx PostgreSQL extension that installs with CREATE EXTENSION and needs no vector-database sidecar. RuVector is broader than that extension: the current developer-facing ruvector 0.2.34 Node package loaded a standalone native N-API vector database through Bun, and no installed LifeOS PostgreSQL extension activation was proven.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-024
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: Current envctl does not select PostgreSQL branch state or reconstruct files from module_path. Its optional semantic adapter treats module_path as vector identity/metadata; current materialization is a separate CodeDB operation.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-025
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: Nix/Yazelix can compile a source directory and CodeDB can separately materialize stored files, but no current end-to-end selected-PostgreSQL-state to materialized directory to isolated Yazelix/Nix build trace exists.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-026
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: redb is passive transactional storage and has no ranking or learning semantics. Any ranking change is owned by application, model, RuVector, SONA, or AgentDB code.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-027
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: The latest developer-facing AgentDB package proves the core integration: agentdb 3.0.0-alpha.17 exposes SelfLearningRvfBackend over .rvf, wraps @ruvector/sona, enhances searches, records trajectories, accepts quality feedback, trains patterns, and a Bun live probe created an AgentDB .rvf plus native SONA learning. Qualification is required because search does not expose the internal q_* trajectory ID expected by recordFeedback; the documented hit-ID call recorded zero trajectories in the live probe, so the complete feedback-to-ranking contract is currently defective.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-028
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: The latest Bun-loaded packages prove every narrower component: RVF defines model/overlay/LoRA segments, native SONA exposes Micro-LoRA, ruvllm held and switched 51 in-memory LoRA adapters, and Tiny Dancer trained and scored a FastGRNN router. They do not prove that one current AgentDB .rvf file persisted both the Micro-LoRA adapters and FastGRNN model, nor that 50+ complete ruvllm personalities hot-swap within one measured hardware footprint.

claim_ids: REDB-CLAIM-029
evidence_type: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
assessment: The defensible boundary is verified: redb can be one portable embedded storage component but is not a geometry engine, vector database, learning system, SQL authority, or complete intelligence architecture. Its elevation to the strategic LifeOS foundation remains an unrecorded owner architecture decision.
